verses.greek: Παῦλος ἀπόστολος ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃ διὰ θελήματος θεοῦ καὶ Τιμόθεος ὁ ἀδελφὸς
verses.mistral: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy, the brother.
verses.kjv: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timotheus our brother,

verses.greek: τοῖς ἐν ⸀Κολοσσαῖς ἁγίοις καὶ πιστοῖς ἀδελφοῖς ἐν Χριστῷ· χάρις ὑμῖν καὶ εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς ⸀ἡμῶν.
verses.mistral: To the saints and faithful brothers in Christ at Colossae: Grace to you and peace from God our Father.
verses.kjv: To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ which are at Colosse: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.greek: Εὐχαριστοῦμεν τῷ ⸀θεῷ πατρὶ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ πάντοτε ⸀περὶ ὑμῶν προσευχόμενοι,
verses.mistral: We give thanks to God the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ always, praying for you
verses.kjv: We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,

verses.greek: ἀκούσαντες τὴν πίστιν ὑμῶν ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ καὶ τὴν ἀγάπην ⸂ἣν ἔχετε⸃ εἰς πάντας τοὺς ἁγίους
verses.mistral: Hearing the faith in Christ Jesus and the love which you have for all saints
verses.kjv: Since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus, and of the love which ye have to all the saints,

verses.greek: διὰ τὴν ἐλπίδα τὴν ἀποκειμένην ὑμῖν ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς, ἣν προηκούσατε ἐν τῷ λόγῳ τῆς ἀληθείας τοῦ εὐαγγελίου
verses.mistral: The hope which is laid up for you in heaven, of which you heard before in the word of truth of the Gospel.
verses.kjv: For the hope which is laid up for you in heaven, whereof ye heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel;

words.gloss: I have been put away, am stored, am reserved for

verses.greek: τοῦ παρόντος εἰς ὑμᾶς, καθὼς καὶ ἐν παντὶ τῷ ⸀κόσμῳ ἐστὶν καρποφορούμενον καὶ αὐξανόμενον καθὼς καὶ ἐν ὑμῖν, ἀφ’ ἧς ἡμέρας ἠκούσατε καὶ ἐπέγνωτε τὴν χάριν τοῦ θεοῦ ἐν ἀληθείᾳ·
verses.mistral: The present that is destined for you, as it is also in the whole world producing fruit and growing, so it is also among you, from which day you heard and knew the grace of God in truth.
verses.kjv: Which is come unto you, as it is in all the world; and bringeth forth fruit, as it doth also in you, since the day ye heard of it, and knew the grace of God in truth:

words.gloss: I cause to increase, become greater, grow
I come to know, discern, recognize

verses.greek: ⸀καθὼς ἐμάθετε ἀπὸ Ἐπαφρᾶ τοῦ ἀγαπητοῦ συνδούλου ἡμῶν, ὅς ἐστιν πιστὸς ὑπὲρ ⸀ἡμῶν διάκονος τοῦ Χριστοῦ,
verses.mistral: According to Epaphras, our beloved fellow servant, who is faithful on our behalf as a minister of Christ
verses.kjv: As ye also learned of Epaphras our dear fellowservant, who is for you a faithful minister of Christ;

verses.greek: ὁ καὶ δηλώσας ἡμῖν τὴν ὑμῶν ἀγάπην ἐν πνεύματι.
verses.mistral: Revealed to us your love in the Spirit.
verses.kjv: Who also declared unto us your love in the Spirit.

verses.greek: Διὰ τοῦτο καὶ ἡμεῖς, ἀφ’ ἧς ἡμέρας ἠκούσαμεν, οὐ παυόμεθα ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν προσευχόμενοι καὶ αἰτούμενοι ἵνα πληρωθῆτε τὴν ἐπίγνωσιν τοῦ θελήματος αὐτοῦ ἐν πάσῃ σοφίᾳ καὶ συνέσει πνευματικῇ,
verses.mistral: We do not cease to pray for you, asking that you may be filled with the knowledge of His will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding.
verses.kjv: For this cause we also, since the day we heard it, do not cease to pray for you, and to desire that ye might be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding;

verses.greek: ⸀περιπατῆσαι ἀξίως τοῦ κυρίου εἰς πᾶσαν ἀρεσκείαν ἐν παντὶ ἔργῳ ἀγαθῷ καρποφοροῦντες καὶ αὐξανόμενοι ⸂τῇ ἐπιγνώσει⸃ τοῦ θεοῦ,
verses.mistral: Walk worthy of the Lord, fully pleasing to Him, bearing fruit in every good work and increasing in the knowledge of God.
verses.kjv: That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing, being fruitful in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;

words.gloss: I cause to increase, become greater, grow

verses.greek: ἐν πάσῃ δυνάμει δυναμούμενοι κατὰ τὸ κράτος τῆς δόξης αὐτοῦ εἰς πᾶσαν ὑπομονὴν καὶ μακροθυμίαν μετὰ χαρᾶς,
verses.mistral: In all power, being strengthened according to the might of His glory, with endurance and patience, with joy.
verses.kjv: Strengthened with all might, according to his glorious power, unto all patience and longsuffering with joyfulness;

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: εὐχαριστοῦντες τῷ πατρὶ τῷ ἱκανώσαντι ⸀ὑμᾶς εἰς τὴν μερίδα τοῦ κλήρου τῶν ἁγίων ἐν τῷ φωτί,
verses.mistral: Thanking the Father who qualified you for a share in the inheritance of the saints in the light
verses.kjv: Giving thanks unto the Father, which hath made us meet to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light:

verses.greek: ὃς ἐρρύσατο ἡμᾶς ἐκ τῆς ἐξουσίας τοῦ σκότους καὶ μετέστησεν εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ υἱοῦ τῆς ἀγάπης αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Translated: Who rescued us from the dominion of darkness and transferred us to the kingdom of the Son of His love.
verses.kjv: Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness, and hath translated us into the kingdom of his dear Son:

words.gloss: I move out of its place, transfer

verses.greek: ἐν ᾧ ἔχομεν τὴν ἀπολύτρωσιν, τὴν ἄφεσιν τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν·
verses.mistral: In whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
verses.kjv: In whom we have redemption through his blood, even the forgiveness of sins:

verses.greek: ὅς ἐστιν εἰκὼν τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ἀοράτου, πρωτότοκος πάσης κτίσεως,
verses.mistral: Image of the invisible God, firstborn of all creation
verses.kjv: Who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature:

verses.greek: ὅτι ἐν αὐτῷ ἐκτίσθη τὰ ⸀πάντα ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς ⸀καὶ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς, τὰ ὁρατὰ καὶ τὰ ἀόρατα, εἴτε θρόνοι εἴτε κυριότητες εἴτε ἀρχαὶ εἴτε ἐξουσίαι· τὰ πάντα δι’ αὐτοῦ καὶ εἰς αὐτὸν ἔκτισται·
verses.mistral: In him all things were created, both in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible, whether thrones or dominions or rulers or authorities—all things were created through him and for him.
verses.kjv: For by him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible, whether they be thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers: all things were created by him, and for him:

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: καὶ αὐτός ἐστιν πρὸ πάντων καὶ τὰ πάντα ἐν αὐτῷ συνέστηκεν,
verses.mistral: He is before all and in him all things hold together.
verses.kjv: And he is before all things, and by him all things consist.

verses.greek: καὶ αὐτός ἐστιν ἡ κεφαλὴ τοῦ σώματος τῆς ἐκκλησίας· ὅς ⸀ἐστιν ἀρχή, πρωτότοκος ἐκ τῶν νεκρῶν, ἵνα γένηται ἐν πᾶσιν αὐτὸς πρωτεύων,
verses.mistral: He is the head of the body, the church; who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, that in all things he might have the preeminence.
verses.kjv: And he is the head of the body, the church: who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ὅτι ἐν αὐτῷ εὐδόκησεν πᾶν τὸ πλήρωμα κατοικῆσαι
verses.mistral: All fullness dwells in him.
verses.kjv: For it pleased the Father that in him should all fulness dwell;

words.gloss: I dwell in, settle in, inhabit

verses.greek: καὶ δι’ αὐτοῦ ἀποκαταλλάξαι τὰ πάντα εἰς αὐτόν, εἰρηνοποιήσας διὰ τοῦ αἵματος τοῦ σταυροῦ αὐτοῦ, ⸂[δι’ αὐτοῦ]⸃ εἴτε τὰ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς εἴτε τὰ ⸀ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς·
verses.mistral: Through Him, He reconciles all things to Himself, making peace through the blood of His cross, whether on earth or in heaven.
verses.kjv: And, having made peace through the blood of his cross, by him to reconcile all things unto himself; by him, I say, whether they be things in earth, or things in heaven.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: καὶ ὑμᾶς ποτε ὄντας ἀπηλλοτριωμένους καὶ ἐχθροὺς τῇ διανοίᾳ ἐν τοῖς ἔργοις τοῖς πονηροῖς—
verses.mistral: And you, who at one time were estranged and enemies in your minds because of your evil actions.
verses.kjv: And you, that were sometime alienated and enemies in your mind by wicked works, yet now hath he reconciled

verses.greek: νυνὶ δὲ ⸀ἀποκατηλλάγητε ἐν τῷ σώματι τῆς σαρκὸς αὐτοῦ διὰ τοῦ θανάτου— παραστῆσαι ὑμᾶς ἁγίους καὶ ἀμώμους καὶ ἀνεγκλήτους κατενώπιον αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Now you have been reconciled in his body of flesh by death, presenting you holy, and blameless, and unreproveable in his sight.
verses.kjv: In the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and unblameable and unreproveable in his sight:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I bring, present, come up to and stand by

verses.greek: εἴ γε ἐπιμένετε τῇ πίστει τεθεμελιωμένοι καὶ ἑδραῖοι καὶ μὴ μετακινούμενοι ἀπὸ τῆς ἐλπίδος τοῦ εὐαγγελίου οὗ ἠκούσατε, τοῦ κηρυχθέντος ἐν ⸀πάσῃ κτίσει τῇ ὑπὸ τὸν οὐρανόν, οὗ ἐγενόμην ἐγὼ Παῦλος διάκονος.
verses.mistral: If you continue in the faith, grounded and steadfast, and not shifting from the hope of the gospel which you heard, which was proclaimed in all creation under heaven, of which I became a servant, Paul.
verses.kjv: If ye continue in the faith grounded and settled, and be not moved away from the hope of the gospel, which ye have heard, and which was preached to every creature which is under heaven; whereof I Paul am made a minister;

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: Νῦν χαίρω ἐν τοῖς παθήμασιν ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν, καὶ ἀνταναπληρῶ τὰ ὑστερήματα τῶν θλίψεων τοῦ Χριστοῦ ἐν τῇ σαρκί μου ὑπὲρ τοῦ σώματος αὐτοῦ, ὅ ἐστιν ἡ ἐκκλησία,
verses.mistral: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for you, and I complete what is lacking in Christ's afflictions in his body on behalf of the church.
verses.kjv: Who now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up that which is behind of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body's sake, which is the church:

words.gloss: I fill up in place of someone else

verses.greek: ἧς ἐγενόμην ἐγὼ διάκονος κατὰ τὴν οἰκονομίαν τοῦ θεοῦ τὴν δοθεῖσάν μοι εἰς ὑμᾶς πληρῶσαι τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ,
verses.mistral: I became a servant of the administration of God, which was given to me for you, to fulfill the word of God.
verses.kjv: Whereof I am made a minister, according to the dispensation of God which is given to me for you, to fulfil the word of God;

words.gloss: I come into being, am born
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: τὸ μυστήριον τὸ ἀποκεκρυμμένον ἀπὸ τῶν αἰώνων καὶ ἀπὸ τῶν γενεῶν,— ⸀νῦν δὲ ἐφανερώθη τοῖς ἁγίοις αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: The mystery hidden from ages and generations has now been revealed to his saints.
verses.kjv: Even the mystery which hath been hid from ages and from generations, but now is made manifest to his saints:

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I make clear, visible, or manifest

verses.greek: οἷς ἠθέλησεν ὁ θεὸς γνωρίσαι τί τὸ πλοῦτος τῆς δόξης τοῦ μυστηρίου τούτου ἐν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν, ⸀ὅ ἐστιν Χριστὸς ἐν ὑμῖν, ἡ ἐλπὶς τῆς δόξης·
verses.mistral: Which God willed to make known among the Gentiles is the gospel of Christ, the riches of glory of this mystery, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
verses.kjv: To whom God would make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; which is Christ in you, the hope of glory:

verses.greek: ὃν ἡμεῖς καταγγέλλομεν νουθετοῦντες πάντα ἄνθρωπον καὶ διδάσκοντες πάντα ἄνθρωπον ἐν πάσῃ σοφίᾳ, ἵνα παραστήσωμεν πάντα ἄνθρωπον τέλειον ἐν ⸀Χριστῷ·
verses.mistral: We are proclaiming and urging all people to live a life worthy of God, who calls you into His own kingdom and glory. Teaching them with all wisdom so that we may present every person mature in Christ.
verses.kjv: Whom we preach, warning every man, and teaching every man in all wisdom; that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus:

words.gloss: I bring, present, come up to and stand by

verses.greek: εἰς ὃ καὶ κοπιῶ ἀγωνιζόμενος κατὰ τὴν ἐνέργειαν αὐτοῦ τὴν ἐνεργουμένην ἐν ἐμοὶ ἐν δυνάμει.
verses.mistral: Striving according to His power which is at work within me.
verses.kjv: Whereunto I also labour, striving according to his working, which worketh in me mightily.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: Θέλω γὰρ ὑμᾶς εἰδέναι ἡλίκον ἀγῶνα ἔχω ⸀ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν καὶ τῶν ἐν Λαοδικείᾳ καὶ ὅσοι οὐχ ἑόρακαν τὸ πρόσωπόν μου ἐν σαρκί,
verses.mistral: I have a struggle on your behalf, and for those in Laodicea, and for all who have not seen my face in the flesh.
verses.kjv: For I would that ye knew what great conflict I have for you, and for them at Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;

verses.greek: ἵνα παρακληθῶσιν αἱ καρδίαι αὐτῶν, ⸀συμβιβασθέντες ἐν ἀγάπῃ καὶ εἰς ⸂πᾶν πλοῦτος⸃ τῆς πληροφορίας τῆς συνέσεως, εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν τοῦ μυστηρίου τοῦ ⸀θεοῦ, Χριστοῦ,
verses.mistral: That they may be encouraged, being knit together in love, attaining to the knowledge of the mystery of God, Christ.
verses.kjv: That their hearts might be comforted, being knit together in love, and unto all riches of the full assurance of understanding, to the acknowledgement of the mystery of God, and of the Father, and of Christ;

verses.greek: ἐν ᾧ εἰσιν πάντες οἱ θησαυροὶ τῆς σοφίας ⸀καὶ γνώσεως ἀπόκρυφοι.
verses.mistral: All the treasures of wisdom and knowledge are hidden in Him.
verses.kjv: In whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.

verses.greek: ⸀τοῦτο λέγω ἵνα ⸀μηδεὶς ὑμᾶς παραλογίζηται ἐν πιθανολογίᾳ.
verses.mistral: This translates to: "That no one may boast in your presence."
verses.kjv: And this I say, lest any man should beguile you with enticing words.

verses.greek: εἰ γὰρ καὶ τῇ σαρκὶ ἄπειμι, ἀλλὰ τῷ πνεύματι σὺν ὑμῖν εἰμι, χαίρων καὶ βλέπων ὑμῶν τὴν τάξιν καὶ τὸ στερέωμα τῆς εἰς Χριστὸν πίστεως ὑμῶν.
verses.mistral: I am with you in spirit, rejoicing and seeing your order and the firmness of your faith in Christ.
verses.kjv: For though I be absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, joying and beholding your order, and the stedfastness of your faith in Christ.

verses.greek: Ὡς οὖν παρελάβετε τὸν Χριστὸν Ἰησοῦν τὸν κύριον, ἐν αὐτῷ περιπατεῖτε,
verses.mistral: In Him walk.
verses.kjv: As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him:

verses.greek: ἐρριζωμένοι καὶ ἐποικοδομούμενοι ἐν αὐτῷ καὶ ⸀βεβαιούμενοι τῇ πίστει καθὼς ἐδιδάχθητε, ⸀περισσεύοντες ἐν εὐχαριστίᾳ.
verses.mistral: Rooted and built up in Him, and established in the faith, just as you were taught, overflowing with gratitude.
verses.kjv: Rooted and built up in him, and stablished in the faith, as ye have been taught, abounding therein with thanksgiving.

verses.greek: Βλέπετε μή τις ὑμᾶς ἔσται ὁ συλαγωγῶν διὰ τῆς φιλοσοφίας καὶ κενῆς ἀπάτης κατὰ τὴν παράδοσιν τῶν ἀνθρώπων, κατὰ τὰ στοιχεῖα τοῦ κόσμου καὶ οὐ κατὰ Χριστόν·
verses.mistral: Do not be carried away by diverse and opposing ideas, which result from human tradition rather than from Christ.
verses.kjv: Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the rudiments of the world, and not after Christ.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
down from, against, according to, throughout, during
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ὅτι ἐν αὐτῷ κατοικεῖ πᾶν τὸ πλήρωμα τῆς θεότητος σωματικῶς,
verses.mistral: In him dwells all the fullness of the godhead bodily.
verses.kjv: For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily.

words.gloss: I dwell in, settle in, inhabit

verses.greek: καὶ ἐστὲ ἐν αὐτῷ πεπληρωμένοι, ὅς ἐστιν ἡ κεφαλὴ πάσης ἀρχῆς καὶ ἐξουσίας,
verses.mistral: You are complete in him, who is the head of all rule and authority.
verses.kjv: And ye are complete in him, which is the head of all principality and power:

verses.greek: ἐν ᾧ καὶ περιετμήθητε περιτομῇ ἀχειροποιήτῳ ἐν τῇ ἀπεκδύσει τοῦ ⸀σώματος τῆς σαρκός, ἐν τῇ περιτομῇ τοῦ Χριστοῦ,
verses.mistral: Circumcised in him was your circumcision made without hands in the taking off of the body of the flesh, in the circumcision of Christ.
verses.kjv: In whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, in putting off the body of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ:

verses.greek: συνταφέντες αὐτῷ ἐν τῷ ⸀βαπτισμῷ, ἐν ᾧ καὶ συνηγέρθητε διὰ τῆς πίστεως τῆς ἐνεργείας τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ἐγείραντος αὐτὸν ⸀ἐκ νεκρῶν·
verses.mistral: In him you were also raised with him, through faith in the working of God, who raised him from the dead.
verses.kjv: Buried with him in baptism, wherein also ye are risen with him through the faith of the operation of God, who hath raised him from the dead.

verses.greek: καὶ ὑμᾶς νεκροὺς ὄντας ⸀ἐν τοῖς παραπτώμασιν καὶ τῇ ἀκροβυστίᾳ τῆς σαρκὸς ὑμῶν, συνεζωοποίησεν ὑμᾶς σὺν αὐτῷ· χαρισάμενος ἡμῖν πάντα τὰ παραπτώματα,
verses.mistral: And you, who were once dead in your transgressions and the uncircumcision of your flesh, He made alive together with him. Grace be to all of us, and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: And you, being dead in your sins and the uncircumcision of your flesh, hath he quickened together with him, having forgiven you all trespasses;

verses.greek: ἐξαλείψας τὸ καθ’ ἡμῶν χειρόγραφον τοῖς δόγμασιν ὃ ἦν ὑπεναντίον ἡμῖν, καὶ αὐτὸ ἦρκεν ἐκ τοῦ μέσου προσηλώσας αὐτὸ τῷ σταυρῷ·
verses.mistral: Erased the handwriting that was against us, which was opposing us, and He disarmed it from the cross.
verses.kjv: Blotting out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us, which was contrary to us, and took it out of the way, nailing it to his cross;

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ἀπεκδυσάμενος τὰς ἀρχὰς καὶ τὰς ἐξουσίας ἐδειγμάτισεν ἐν παρρησίᾳ, θριαμβεύσας αὐτοὺς ἐν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: Disarmed the authorities and the powers, He displayed them openly, triumphing over them in Him.
verses.kjv: And having spoiled principalities and powers, he made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them in it.

words.gloss: I hold up as an example
I lead around, make a spectacle of

verses.greek: Μὴ οὖν τις ὑμᾶς κρινέτω ἐν βρώσει ⸀ἢ ἐν πόσει ἢ ἐν μέρει ἑορτῆς ἢ νουμηνίας ἢ σαββάτων,
verses.mistral: Do not let anyone judge you in food or in drink, or regarding a festival or a new moon or Sabbaths.
verses.kjv: Let no man therefore judge you in meat, or in drink, or in respect of an holyday, or of the new moon, or of the sabbath days:

verses.greek: ἅ ἐστιν σκιὰ τῶν μελλόντων, τὸ δὲ σῶμα ⸀τοῦ Χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: The shadow of things to come; but the substance is of Christ.
verses.kjv: Which are a shadow of things to come; but the body is of Christ.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: μηδεὶς ὑμᾶς καταβραβευέτω θέλων ἐν ταπεινοφροσύνῃ καὶ θρησκείᾳ τῶν ἀγγέλων, ⸀ἃ ἑόρακεν ἐμβατεύων, εἰκῇ φυσιούμενος ὑπὸ τοῦ νοὸς τῆς σαρκὸς αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: No one must intimidate you with empty words based on human philosophy and the religion of angels, which he claims to have seen in visions, being merely a reflection of the fleshly nature of his own mind.
verses.kjv: Let no man beguile you of your reward in a voluntary humility and worshipping of angels, intruding into those things which he hath not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,

verses.greek: καὶ οὐ κρατῶν τὴν κεφαλήν, ἐξ οὗ πᾶν τὸ σῶμα διὰ τῶν ἁφῶν καὶ συνδέσμων ἐπιχορηγούμενον καὶ συμβιβαζόμενον αὔξει τὴν αὔξησιν τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: And he is not holding the head, from which all the body by means of joints and bands grows with a growth of God.
verses.kjv: And not holding the Head, from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.

verses.greek: Εἰ ἀπεθάνετε σὺν Χριστῷ ἀπὸ τῶν στοιχείων τοῦ κόσμου, τί ὡς ζῶντες ἐν κόσμῳ δογματίζεσθε·
verses.mistral: If you have died with Christ, why are you being made subject to rules as if you were still in the world?
verses.kjv: Wherefore if ye be dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why, as though living in the world, are ye subject to ordinances,

words.gloss: I am dying, am about to die

verses.greek: Μὴ ἅψῃ μηδὲ γεύσῃ μηδὲ θίγῃς,
verses.mistral: Do not touch, do not taste, do not handle
verses.kjv: (Touch not; taste not; handle not;

verses.greek: ἅ ἐστιν πάντα εἰς φθορὰν τῇ ἀποχρήσει, κατὰ τὰ ἐντάλματα καὶ διδασκαλίας τῶν ἀνθρώπων;
verses.mistral: Everything that perishes with the use, according to human commandments and teachings?
verses.kjv: Which all are to perish with the using;) after the commandments and doctrines of men?

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ἅτινά ἐστιν λόγον μὲν ἔχοντα σοφίας ἐν ἐθελοθρησκίᾳ καὶ ταπεινοφροσύνῃ καὶ ἀφειδίᾳ σώματος, οὐκ ἐν τιμῇ τινι πρὸς πλησμονὴν τῆς σαρκός.
verses.mistral: Wisdom, with understanding in will-worship, humility, and unwavering endurance of the body, is not acceptable to the flesh for its gratification.
verses.kjv: Which things have indeed a shew of wisdom in will worship, and humility, and neglecting of the body; not in any honour to the satisfying of the flesh.

verses.greek: Εἰ οὖν συνηγέρθητε τῷ Χριστῷ, τὰ ἄνω ζητεῖτε, οὗ ὁ Χριστός ἐστιν ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ θεοῦ καθήμενος·
verses.mistral: If then you were raised with Christ, seek the things that are above, where Christ is seated at the right hand of God.
verses.kjv: If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.

verses.greek: τὰ ἄνω φρονεῖτε, μὴ τὰ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς,
verses.mistral: Consider the things that are above, not the things on earth.
verses.kjv: Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ἀπεθάνετε γάρ, καὶ ἡ ζωὴ ὑμῶν κέκρυπται σὺν τῷ Χριστῷ ἐν τῷ θεῷ·
verses.mistral: Have died, indeed, and your life hidden with Christ in God.
verses.kjv: For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.

words.gloss: I am dying, am about to die

verses.greek: ὅταν ὁ Χριστὸς φανερωθῇ, ἡ ζωὴ ⸀ὑμῶν, τότε καὶ ὑμεῖς σὺν αὐτῷ φανερωθήσεσθε ἐν δόξῃ.
verses.mistral: When Christ appears, then you also will appear with Him in glory.
verses.kjv: When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.

words.gloss: I make clear, visible, or manifest
I make clear, visible, or manifest

verses.greek: Νεκρώσατε οὖν τὰ ⸀μέλη τὰ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς, πορνείαν, ἀκαθαρσίαν, πάθος, ἐπιθυμίαν κακήν, καὶ τὴν πλεονεξίαν ἥτις ἐστὶν εἰδωλολατρία,
verses.mistral: Mortify therefore the members which are upon the earth: fornication, uncleanness, passion, evil desire, and covetousness, which is idolatry.
verses.kjv: Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness, which is idolatry:

words.gloss: I put to death, render weak
on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: δι’ ἃ ἔρχεται ἡ ὀργὴ τοῦ θεοῦ ⸂ἐπὶ τοὺς υἱοὺς τῆς ἀπειθείας⸃·
verses.mistral: God's wrath comes upon the children of disobedience.
verses.kjv: For which things' sake the wrath of God cometh on the children of disobedience:

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at

verses.greek: ἐν οἷς καὶ ὑμεῖς περιεπατήσατέ ποτε ὅτε ἐζῆτε ἐν ⸀τούτοις·
verses.mistral: In whom you also once lived (The Greek phrase is: "In quibus et vos periepatesate pothete, hoc est, in his ubi quaeratis.")
verses.kjv: In the which ye also walked some time, when ye lived in them.

verses.greek: νυνὶ δὲ ἀπόθεσθε καὶ ὑμεῖς τὰ πάντα, ὀργήν, θυμόν, κακίαν, βλασφημίαν, αἰσχρολογίαν ἐκ τοῦ στόματος ὑμῶν·
verses.mistral: Discard all (things)—anger, wrath, malice, slander, and obscene talk from your mouth.
verses.kjv: But now ye also put off all these; anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy communication out of your mouth.

words.gloss: but, on the other hand, and
I lay off or aside, renounce

verses.greek: μὴ ψεύδεσθε εἰς ἀλλήλους· ἀπεκδυσάμενοι τὸν παλαιὸν ἄνθρωπον σὺν ταῖς πράξεσιν αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Do not lie to one another. Putting off the old self with its practices.
verses.kjv: Lie not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the old man with his deeds;

verses.greek: καὶ ἐνδυσάμενοι τὸν νέον τὸν ἀνακαινούμενον εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν κατ’ εἰκόνα τοῦ κτίσαντος αὐτόν,
verses.mistral: Put on the new self, created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness.
verses.kjv: And have put on the new man, which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him:

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ὅπου οὐκ ἔνι Ἕλλην καὶ Ἰουδαῖος, περιτομὴ καὶ ἀκροβυστία, βάρβαρος, Σκύθης, δοῦλος, ἐλεύθερος, ἀλλὰ ⸀τὰ πάντα καὶ ἐν πᾶσιν Χριστός.
verses.mistral: In all and in each, Christ.
verses.kjv: Where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond nor free: but Christ is all, and in all.

verses.greek: Ἐνδύσασθε οὖν ὡς ἐκλεκτοὶ τοῦ θεοῦ, ἅγιοι καὶ ἠγαπημένοι, σπλάγχνα οἰκτιρμοῦ, χρηστότητα, ταπεινοφροσύνην, πραΰτητα, μακροθυμίαν,
verses.mistral: Clothe yourselves with compassion, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience. Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you. And over all these virtues put on love, which binds them all together in perfect unity. However, for your specific request, the Greek phrase " , , , , , , " translates to: "Clothe yourselves with the attributes of God's chosen ones, being holy and loving, compassionate, kind, humble, gentle, patient, bearing with one another and forgiving each other."
verses.kjv: Put on therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, bowels of mercies, kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering;

verses.greek: ἀνεχόμενοι ἀλλήλων καὶ χαριζόμενοι ἑαυτοῖς ἐάν τις πρός τινα ἔχῃ μομφήν· καθὼς καὶ ὁ ⸀κύριος ἐχαρίσατο ὑμῖν οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς·
verses.mistral: Forgive each other, just as the Lord forgave you.
verses.kjv: Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye.

verses.greek: ἐπὶ πᾶσιν δὲ τούτοις τὴν ἀγάπην, ⸀ὅ ἐστιν σύνδεσμος τῆς τελειότητος.
verses.mistral: Upon these things, love; for it is the bond of perfection.
verses.kjv: And above all these things put on charity, which is the bond of perfectness.

words.gloss: on, to, against, on the basis of, at
but, on the other hand, and

verses.greek: καὶ ἡ εἰρήνη τοῦ ⸀Χριστοῦ βραβευέτω ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις ὑμῶν, εἰς ἣν καὶ ἐκλήθητε ἐν ἑνὶ σώματι· καὶ εὐχάριστοι γίνεσθε.
verses.mistral: And the peace of Christ be with you, which also ye were called in one body: and be ye thankful.
verses.kjv: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body; and be ye thankful.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ὁ λόγος τοῦ Χριστοῦ ἐνοικείτω ἐν ὑμῖν πλουσίως ἐν πάσῃ σοφίᾳ· διδάσκοντες καὶ νουθετοῦντες ἑαυτοὺς ⸀ψαλμοῖς, ⸀ὕμνοις, ᾠδαῖς πνευματικαῖς ⸀ἐν χάριτι, ᾄδοντες ἐν ⸂ταῖς καρδίαις⸃ ὑμῶν τῷ ⸀θεῷ·
verses.mistral: The Word of Christ dwells in you richly, in every wisdom. Teaching and admonishing one another in spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to God.
verses.kjv: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.

words.gloss: I dwell in, am settled in

verses.greek: καὶ πᾶν ⸂ὅ τι⸃ ⸀ἐὰν ποιῆτε ἐν λόγῳ ἢ ἐν ἔργῳ, πάντα ἐν ὀνόματι κυρίου Ἰησοῦ, εὐχαριστοῦντες τῷ ⸀θεῷ πατρὶ δι’ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: In the name of Jesus Christ, give thanks to the Father.
verses.kjv: And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.

verses.greek: Αἱ γυναῖκες, ὑποτάσσεσθε ⸀τοῖς ἀνδράσιν, ὡς ἀνῆκεν ἐν κυρίῳ.
verses.mistral: Women, submit to your husbands as is fitting in the Lord.
verses.kjv: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord.

verses.greek: οἱ ἄνδρες, ἀγαπᾶτε τὰς γυναῖκας καὶ μὴ πικραίνεσθε πρὸς αὐτάς.
verses.mistral: Men, love your wives and do not be bitter towards them.
verses.kjv: Husbands, love your wives, and be not bitter against them.

verses.greek: Τὰ τέκνα, ὑπακούετε τοῖς γονεῦσιν κατὰ πάντα, τοῦτο γὰρ ⸂εὐάρεστόν ἐστιν⸃ ἐν κυρίῳ.
verses.mistral: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this is acceptable in the Lord.
verses.kjv: Children, obey your parents in all things: for this is well pleasing unto the Lord.

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: οἱ πατέρες, μὴ ἐρεθίζετε τὰ τέκνα ὑμῶν, ἵνα μὴ ἀθυμῶσιν.
verses.mistral: Fathers, do not provoke your children, lest they become disheartened.
verses.kjv: Fathers, provoke not your children to anger, lest they be discouraged.

verses.greek: οἱ δοῦλοι, ὑπακούετε κατὰ πάντα τοῖς κατὰ σάρκα κυρίοις, μὴ ἐν ⸀ὀφθαλμοδουλίαις, ὡς ἀνθρωπάρεσκοι, ἀλλ’ ἐν ἁπλότητι καρδίας, φοβούμενοι τὸν ⸀κύριον.
verses.mistral: Obey in all things the earthly masters, not in service to human passions, but with simplicity of heart, fearing the Lord.
verses.kjv: Servants, obey in all things your masters according to the flesh; not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but in singleness of heart, fearing God:

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ⸀ὃ ἐὰν ποιῆτε, ἐκ ψυχῆς ἐργάζεσθε, ὡς τῷ κυρίῳ καὶ οὐκ ἀνθρώποις,
verses.mistral: Whatever you do, do from the heart, as to the Lord and not to men.
verses.kjv: And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men;

verses.greek: εἰδότες ὅτι ἀπὸ κυρίου ⸀ἀπολήμψεσθε τὴν ἀνταπόδοσιν τῆς κληρονομίας· ⸀τῷ κυρίῳ Χριστῷ δουλεύετε·
verses.mistral: Receiving the reward of the inheritance from the Lord, serve the Lord Christ.
verses.kjv: Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.

words.gloss: I am a slave, am subject to, obey

verses.greek: ὁ ⸀γὰρ ἀδικῶν κομίσεται ὃ ἠδίκησεν, καὶ οὐκ ἔστιν προσωπολημψία.
verses.mistral: The wrongdoer will receive what they deserve, and there is no room for partiality.
verses.kjv: But he that doeth wrong shall receive for the wrong which he hath done: and there is no respect of persons.

verses.greek: οἱ κύριοι, τὸ δίκαιον καὶ τὴν ἰσότητα τοῖς δούλοις παρέχεσθε, εἰδότες ὅτι καὶ ὑμεῖς ἔχετε κύριον ἐν ⸀οὐρανῷ.
verses.mistral: Lords, the righteousness and equality are given to the slaves, knowing that you also have a Lord in heaven.
verses.kjv: Masters, give unto your servants that which is just and equal; knowing that ye also have a Master in heaven.

verses.greek: Τῇ προσευχῇ προσκαρτερεῖτε, γρηγοροῦντες ἐν αὐτῇ ἐν εὐχαριστίᾳ,
verses.mistral: Pray in it, being vigilant in it with thankfulness
verses.kjv: Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving;

words.gloss: I am awake, am vigilant, watch

verses.greek: προσευχόμενοι ἅμα καὶ περὶ ἡμῶν, ἵνα ὁ θεὸς ἀνοίξῃ ἡμῖν θύραν τοῦ λόγου, λαλῆσαι τὸ μυστήριον τοῦ Χριστοῦ, δι’ ὃ καὶ δέδεμαι,
verses.mistral: Pray for us also, that God may open to us the door of faith, and reveal the mystery of Christ, in whom I am bound.
verses.kjv: Withal praying also for us, that God would open unto us a door of utterance, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds:

words.gloss: at the same time, along with

verses.greek: ἵνα φανερώσω αὐτὸ ὡς δεῖ με λαλῆσαι.
verses.mistral: Reveal it to me, as I ought to speak.
verses.kjv: That I may make it manifest, as I ought to speak.

words.gloss: I make clear, visible, or manifest

verses.greek: Ἐν σοφίᾳ περιπατεῖτε πρὸς τοὺς ἔξω, τὸν καιρὸν ἐξαγοραζόμενοι.
verses.mistral: Walk wisely towards outsiders, redeeming the time.
verses.kjv: Walk in wisdom toward them that are without, redeeming the time.

verses.greek: ὁ λόγος ὑμῶν πάντοτε ἐν χάριτι, ἅλατι ἠρτυμένος, εἰδέναι πῶς δεῖ ὑμᾶς ἑνὶ ἑκάστῳ ἀποκρίνεσθαι.
verses.mistral: Your message in English is: "Your word in everything should be in grace, seasoned with salt, to know how each one of you ought to answer."
verses.kjv: Let your speech be alway with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.

verses.greek: Τὰ κατ’ ἐμὲ πάντα γνωρίσει ὑμῖν Τυχικὸς ὁ ἀγαπητὸς ἀδελφὸς καὶ πιστὸς διάκονος καὶ σύνδουλος ἐν κυρίῳ,
verses.mistral: Tychicus, the beloved brother and faithful servant in the Lord, will make known to you all things that concern me.
verses.kjv: All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you, who is a beloved brother, and a faithful minister and fellowservant in the Lord:

words.gloss: down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: ὃν ἔπεμψα πρὸς ὑμᾶς εἰς αὐτὸ τοῦτο ἵνα ⸀γνῶτε τὰ περὶ ⸀ἡμῶν καὶ παρακαλέσῃ τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν,
verses.mistral: Knowing the things concerning us, and admonishing you to have a change of heart
verses.kjv: Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that he might know your estate, and comfort your hearts;

words.gloss: I come to know, learn, realize

verses.greek: σὺν Ὀνησίμῳ τῷ πιστῷ καὶ ἀγαπητῷ ἀδελφῷ, ὅς ἐστιν ἐξ ὑμῶν· πάντα ὑμῖν γνωρίσουσιν τὰ ὧδε.
verses.mistral: With Onesimus the faithful and beloved brother, who is among you, all these things will be made known to you.
verses.kjv: With Onesimus, a faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They shall make known unto you all things which are done here.

verses.greek: Ἀσπάζεται ὑμᾶς Ἀρίσταρχος ὁ συναιχμάλωτός μου, καὶ Μᾶρκος ὁ ἀνεψιὸς Βαρναβᾶ (περὶ οὗ ἐλάβετε ἐντολάς, ἐὰν ἔλθῃ πρὸς ὑμᾶς δέξασθε αὐτόν),
verses.mistral: Greets you Aristarchus, my fellow-prisoner, and Mark the cousin of Barnabas (whom you received commands about, if he comes to you, receive him).
verses.kjv: Aristarchus my fellowprisoner saluteth you, and Marcus, sister's son to Barnabas, (touching whom ye received commandments: if he come unto you, receive him;)

words.gloss: I greet, salute, pay my respects to

verses.greek: καὶ Ἰησοῦς ὁ λεγόμενος Ἰοῦστος, οἱ ὄντες ἐκ περιτομῆς, οὗτοι μόνοι συνεργοὶ εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ, οἵτινες ἐγενήθησάν μοι παρηγορία.
verses.mistral: And Jesus, who is called Justus, who are of the circumcision, these only are fellow workers for the kingdom of God, who have been comfort to me.
verses.kjv: And Jesus, which is called Justus, who are of the circumcision. These only are my fellowworkers unto the kingdom of God, which have been a comfort unto me.

words.gloss: I come into being, am born

verses.greek: ἀσπάζεται ὑμᾶς Ἐπαφρᾶς ὁ ἐξ ὑμῶν, δοῦλος ⸀Χριστοῦ, πάντοτε ἀγωνιζόμενος ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν ἐν ταῖς προσευχαῖς, ἵνα ⸀σταθῆτε τέλειοι καὶ ⸀πεπληροφορημένοι ἐν παντὶ θελήματι τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Greets you Epaphras, who is from among you, a servant of Christ. Always laboring on your behalf in prayers, that you may stand perfect and fully knowing the will of God.
verses.kjv: Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, saluteth you, always labouring fervently for you in prayers, that ye may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.

words.gloss: I greet, salute, pay my respects to
I carry out fully, fully convince

verses.greek: μαρτυρῶ γὰρ αὐτῷ ὅτι ἔχει ⸂πολὺν πόνον⸃ ὑπὲρ ὑμῶν καὶ τῶν ἐν Λαοδικείᾳ καὶ τῶν ἐν Ἱεραπόλει.
verses.mistral: He has a great labor on your behalf, and for Laodiceans and those in Hierapolis.
verses.kjv: For I bear him record, that he hath a great zeal for you, and them that are in Laodicea, and them in Hierapolis.

verses.greek: ἀσπάζεται ὑμᾶς Λουκᾶς ὁ ἰατρὸς ὁ ἀγαπητὸς καὶ Δημᾶς.
verses.mistral: Greets you Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas.
verses.kjv: Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas, greet you.

words.gloss: I greet, salute, pay my respects to

verses.greek: ἀσπάσασθε τοὺς ἐν Λαοδικείᾳ ἀδελφοὺς καὶ ⸂Νύμφαν καὶ τὴν κατ’ οἶκον αὐτῆς⸃ ἐκκλησίαν.
verses.mistral: Greet the brothers in Laodicea, Nympha and the church in her house.
verses.kjv: Salute the brethren which are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the church which is in his house.

words.gloss: I greet, salute, pay my respects to
down from, against, according to, throughout, during

verses.greek: καὶ ὅταν ἀναγνωσθῇ παρ’ ὑμῖν ἡ ἐπιστολή, ποιήσατε ἵνα καὶ ἐν τῇ ⸀Λαοδικέων ἐκκλησίᾳ ἀναγνωσθῇ, καὶ τὴν ἐκ Λαοδικείας ἵνα καὶ ὑμεῖς ἀναγνῶτε.
verses.mistral: And when the epistle is read among you, let it also be read in the Laodicean church, and that from Laodicea let it be read by you.
verses.kjv: And when this epistle is read among you, cause that it be read also in the church of the Laodiceans; and that ye likewise read the epistle from Laodicea.

verses.greek: καὶ εἴπατε Ἀρχίππῳ· Βλέπε τὴν διακονίαν ἣν παρέλαβες ἐν κυρίῳ, ἵνα αὐτὴν πληροῖς.
verses.mistral: Speak to Archippus: "Behold the service which you received in the Lord, fulfill it."
verses.kjv: And say to Archippus, Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it.

verses.greek: Ὁ ἀσπασμὸς τῇ ἐμῇ χειρὶ Παύλου. μνημονεύετέ μου τῶν δεσμῶν. ἡ χάρις μεθ’ ⸀ὑμῶν.
verses.mistral: Greetings from me, Paul. Remember my chains. Grace be with you.
verses.kjv: The salutation by the hand of me Paul. Remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen.
